reviews.review_text: We have been travelling in Tasmania now for over two weeks. We booked one of the cabins for 4 nights. Our mistake and we should have checked it out first.|These cabins are small and I mean small. There is nowhere comfortable to sit. The bathroom is tiny. The shower again is tiny but has awesome pressure. One of the best we have had.|The internet is frustratingly slow and you have to put in a new code each day.|The campground has a lot of permanent people staying so there is a lot of traffic all hours.|Because of the lack of space and comfort along with rubbish internet we decided to cut our stay short.|Refund was refused. So I’ll take that on the chin.|To give a comparison, we are staying at the Mercure Hotel in Launceston for around $150 per night and the room is bigger than the unit at this park. We have coffee machines, huge bathroom, top quality bed, comfortable seating, walking distance to town. Fantastic.|Have a good look at this place before you book. |A disappointing stay.
Good place to stay for the SOT or to look around Devonport. You can watch the boats come in and out from your van. Walk tracks all the way into town.

We booked a Deluxe Cabin and did not get a Deluxe Cabin. Our cabin was extremely basic,  the worst we ever had in our travels.  Did not have a fly screen sliding door, the floor fan was not working, the fan on the wall above the bed was not working properly, the small fridge in the room had a missing rail on the door so could not fit much in the fridge door panel, the wifi was not accessible in the cabin, was told it only works intermittently if you stand outside the laundry. |The towel rail fell off the wall because of loose screws.|When I asked at reception why I haven’t been given a Deluxe Cabin  as was booked on Booking.com the person said they were all booked and none were available for me. I showed her my booking receipt which clearly showed I booked a Deluxe cabin and she just shrugged her shoulders and said that none are available.|So don’t trust this mob. I didn’t get what I paid for and she didn’t care.
Super clean and tidy campsite available at short notice. Friendly staff and all guests were well behaved and respectful. Will definitely be returning in the future.

Stayed at an unpowered site, it was fine, but regretted the $35 spent for two people.

- need a key to access all amenities and exit gates
- wifi didn't work the whole time "sometimes does, sometimes doesn't"
- 4 of the 7 women's toilets were out of order (seems to have been that way for a month based on other reviews)
- bathrooms were clean except for a bit of mold through the men's
- super convenient for the Spirit but the boats do honk and cargo loading echos through the park overnight
- location feels pretty rough

Even though I put 4 stars,we still enjoyed our stay here. We have stayed before. Some cabins are getting old & tired.  But comfortable enough.  Absolutely love the closeness of the ocean . Seeing the Spirit of Tasmania coming & going is great. !!
Great cabin, clean and comfortable. Had its own carport. No air-conditioning though.
Good place to land after the ferrey, clean ammenites and located close enough to main part of Devonport by push bike
Good stop over after a long ferry.
Great service and location
Very close to SOT dock, right on the water and close to all you need.

We briefly entered the campsite by car to observe the grounds and overall cleanliness, as any potential customer would do before booking.
Within minutes, reception approached us after being alerted by long-term residents, treating us as intruders rather than prospective guests.

The staff’s attitude was cold, condescending, and openly judgmental. We were explicitly told that “backpackers” were not welcome, associated with being dirty and disrespectful — a sweeping and insulting generalization.
No attempt was made to listen, explain, or show basic courtesy.

This establishment appears to operate as a closed community, where people are categorized and rejected based on assumptions rather than behavior.
If you do not fit their preferred profile, expect hostility instead of hospitality.

An experience that was both humiliating and entirely unprofessional.
Certainly not a place that reflects the values of tourism or respect.

Haven't left yet. No complaints. The proprietors were amazingly helpful to French travellers with language barrier who had broken down. Very handy to Spirit of Tasmania terminal. Clean tidy amenities. Right on the beach with spectacular views. Dog friendly. Secure and felt safe.

Beautiful place right by water conviently located near airport and boats to cross back over beautiful tidy cabins, Arron was very accommodating with my flight rerouting  and changing  dates  as I needed, when I couldn't make it in, he went above and beyond!  I Will definately be staying here again in the future thanks guys!
Nice little van park right on the mouth of the Merset River at Devonport. Bit dated but still has everything you need.
The room was a bit out of date and need some work

Very dated caravan park, but cheap.
Poor signage on the caravan sites so had to get help from a permanent resident
Great facilities, beautiful cabins... easy 5 stars.

Easy to get to from the boat, but if coming at night to non powered sites we aware of large stumps. fantastic shower and toilet facilities, very well kept. loved the spot to hang the key 🗝️. Definately stay again.

We had a great stay. The cabin was super clean and we had all the kitchen facilities we needed. The heating was cozy and there was an extra space heater. The bed was fantastic and the bedding was clean and crisp like a top notch hotel. Shani was very helpful and friendly and we will definitely be back! 👍👍👍
Great for a quick getaway
Stored our caravan here while we visited the east coast of TAS before heading back to the mainland.
No hot shower at all
Nothing gets fixed and all the electrical appliances are out of date for testing 07/2018 last test date beware for plugging into anything
Extremely handy location to get to when off the boat. The owner came out to greet us even though it was after hour's. Wide area's for your caravan.

The cabin I was in was clean and tidy.   Air conditioning wasn't an option,  didn't have it.  Circulation fan in lounge doesn't work and the heater also doesn't work.
Grounds are well kept.

Great location for the spirit of Tasmania.
Cindy was extremely helpful and a pleasure to meet
Very convenient location to Spirit of Tasmania. Amenities clean but run down. One toilet block had cold showers and other block had out of order toilets. Been like this for months
4 minutes of hot water. No face washer or bathroom towel. Comfy enough for 1 or 2 nites. Close to spirit dock. Hot water runs out pretty fast.
Excellent location and good value. Amenities were clean and modern.
We stayed in our own caravan.

We are surprised that the cabin has no air conditioning only 2 very useless fans. This is the 1st cabin we have come across it is a shock we have traveled from North Moonta S.A  to here hope the cabin s get better
A lovely caravan park for a relaxing sojourn.
Great little park so close to the Spirit of tas dock. We got in late so it was perfect. Quiet and plenty of room. The amenities were old but very clean. Great shower pressure!!
It's my home away from home very nice place
Close to sort,only some showers working
Great location, affordable, clean and comfortable.
Close to the Spirit of Tasmania. Convenient to Geelong City.

Great value in a good spot "away" from noisy trucks jake braking and thundering on bumpy spots on the road nearby as one trys to sleep at night.

In the older cabin lower down in the park, portable heater in cabin 11 didnt work, no soap with towels on bed. Otherwise all is great.
Very accommodating as we got in late with our motor home. Very close to where the Spirit of Tasmania docks. Had a powered site here . Amenities include toilets showers and a camp kitchen.

Stayed at the Cabins for work. They were comfortable and the location was convenient. 10min away from town and 10min away from my work.
The staff at the park were super helpful and understanding. They were happy to assist.
Always so handy when we arrive or depart on the Spirit! So helpful and friendly
Good staff.

So close to spirit of Tasmania docking.  Friendly staff.
Great place to stay
Great place in Davenport for an overnight or two, before exploring or getting ready to head back to the mainland on the SOT

I'm sorry but it was an exceptionally hot few days, my cabin had no air conditioning, there was a stench coming from the bathroom! It was close to Spirit of Tasmania and the staff were nice. But for $125 I will never stay there again.
Great value for a clean and comfortable cabin.
Top spot,
Just had a flu jab - painless and comes with a nice chocolate biscuit 🍪
Very nice sports centre and had a very nice market operating

So I went there for an athletics camp, it seemed pretty average, I was playing outside by myself because my friends were being ipad kids after training and then these dudes on bikes and hoods zoomed in and GRABBED me, and they took me to their secret hide out, they lifted their hoods ... and they turned out to be ... teenage mutant eshay turtles. But then I tried to call my manager but guess what? There was no WiFi! Seriously! So I fished through my bag and pulled out my jet and flew out of their base and ordered my fleet of bomber jets to bomb the eshay turtles. I flew back, I may or may not have driven into the girls cabin building! Ppft, no wonder im giving it 1 star like it shouldn't have been right where I was about to land my 10 billion dollar private jet! Like wow. And then we had COLD pizza for dinner. And it was COLD pizza with PINEAPPLE. I just cant. I called my fleet of bomber jets to bomb it. Good RIDDANCE to that place!
What an awesome place, pity the launceston city council didn't take some notice

Excellent market.... but then thats all id ever go for... and maybe I wont particularly once I found out that the entire banksia venue including pathways do not permit dogs, even to walk through... entirely dog unfriendly... so as a result I wont provide my custom to a place that  is that stringent..  Latrobe Council  are one of the most dog unfriendly councils...
A really good market, well arranged.

The new Banksia Markets were fabulous! So much variety from so many talented artists and creators.
Looking forward to the next one!
Tracy 😊

Staff were extremely helpful  with advice and information and nothing was a trouble. The venue is VERY user friendly, very clean, all equipment needed was easily accessible. We held a great family reunion recently so THANK YOU.
Great park suitable for ages. Lots of nature climbing and play to support the see Saws and swings etc. The only issue is that there were no covered table and chair areas for morning tea prep
It's a good park for the kids to play at.
Had a bbq here for Australia day, the place is amazing with lots of play stuff for the kids and the free BBQ was and clean
Multiple play areas and picnic shelters, toilets, water fountain
Great venue for your sporting needs, and an awesome new play ground for children

Stayed here on our first night from the main land
Lovely reception from the owners personally
Was chatting at camp they were helpful with things to do and see
Very clean amenities and laundry
Even booked for 1st of February and forgot to cancel booking they were even kind enough to waiv the fees
Would highly recommend this as a first get to know tassie first before your trip
Thanks 👍 😊

We stayed here as a group of 20+ campers, after a week hiking at Cradle Mountain, before boarding the Spirit of Tasmania to Geelong (a ten minute walk from the caravan park).||Hot showers in clean amenities blocks, a grassy spot to pitch our tents, intel on good, local pizza delivery, super affordable and friendly service. Couldn't ask for more than that!

Stayed the night before we boarded the Spirit of Tasmania.  The location was good for this. It took about 5 mins to the terminal.

It is an older park.  The unpowered sites are not set out. They have a large area (paddock like) where everyone finds a space and sets up.  It was very windy and busy when we were there and we were  concerned about our morning departure being blocked by other campers.
I'd give this place 1/2 Stars if I could. Tourists stay away, the Host is possibly one of The rudest people and should not be in this industry.  Hideous run down over priced dump.
Everything we needed.
Great friendly staff grate place to stay   we stay here again
Man this is a fabulous little nook next to the beach in Devonport.  So affordable for the location.  Clean, tidy and comfy.  Uber delivers here but everything you need is close enough.

Absolutely 💯 %
The lovely caretakers( forgot their names) were extremely helpful in making my time comfortable.. I definitely would stop there again. Pending the same people were caring for the park  . No one couldn't have asked for better kindness & Help
Great park old but clean and quiet nice grass sites should be kept a secret so it stays like it is
Very quiet park ideal if you are coming to Tasmania and docking late

We had the pleasure of staying here for 3 nights
Was lovely and clean close to things
Quiet but kid friendly
The cabin we were in had everything you required including facilities to cook if required
My friends and I walk past with our dogs every morning, looks very nice, everything is very neat and tidy outside the cabins, so they would have to be the same in the little huts

The park itself is pleasant enough. The cabin was meant to be a delux , however,  it was a
Spars.  With oly a plug in two plate cooker and a microwave oven,  and little in the way of cooking equipment.   There is a small table and two chairs to sit and eat and two outdoor chairs instead of a sofa, most unsuitable.  The bed very comfortable other amenities we satisfactory.  Would be very hot in summer as there is no cooling.  If they put a sofa in and a ceiling fan this would make a difference.  Apart from that i would if needs be stay again.
Comfortable,  however,  should have a sofa not two out door chairs.
Clean and quiet place getting off the Spirit of Tas, Staff where very helpful with letting us stay the night and answer the phone after hours 10/10 customer service
Fantastic park to stay at! Such a great location with beautiful grounds. Everything was extremely clean and the staff very lovely. Highly recommend
Nice size wedding done and dusted, very good hall.top job to all of the people involved ❤️

Fantastic week at the Port Sorell caravan park.
Dean the caretaker was very friendly and obliging. The site was large and all amenities spotless. Great location opposite the jetty and beach. Many things to see and do in the surrounding area.
Would thoroughly recommend.

Clean and well looked after caravan park. The amenities are spotlessly clean with easy access. The laundry also was spotless.
Dean, the caretaker is always very friendly and accommodating. Caravan sites are large, close to walking/bike track and beach. Amazing that this park has a 11am checkout 👏👏

We stayed at Port Sorell Caravan Park with our caravan and had a wonderful stay. The park was clean, quiet and well maintained, with spacious sites and a great location close to the beach and a short walk to shops. The staff were friendly and welcoming, and the relaxed atmosphere made it a perfect place to unwind. We would definitely stay again and highly recommend it.
Great campsite, super friendly, very nice views only little thing is that you only get hot water for 2 minutes in the shower.

Dean was accommodating with my request for a late checkout to fit with the Spirit’s night sailing.
The amenities were clean & functional.
The van site was large & relatively flat & level.
The entry road could be repaired & widened.

Lots of great things to say about this park. We had a large unpowered site. Quiet spot tucked away but close to the walking path and river. Staff were very helpful. An easy 20mins from the spirit of Tassie ferry.

Weekend stay over in a caravan, never used the ammenities for showering but from what I saw they had a very new looking ammenities block that looked fantastic.. great location… lovely park… Dean the caretaker is fantastic, always organised, we were a late check in and he had everything ready for us on arrival… only a suggestion is to have a sensor open the boom gate on departure rather than having to use you key fob
Ones again it is a amazing stay out there and good for the sole. I love the peace

Dean was very helpful, friendly and had a very professional manner which we thoroughly appreciated. The caravan park was so peaceful and amenities very clean and modern . Would recommend to all travellers

We have stayed here a couple of times and it is a lovely location. We stayed in our van recently and had a couple of things that weren’t great. Other guests need to use camping etiquette eg dogs are required to stay on lead, sadly this did not happen and kept coming and upsetting our dog. Quiet time is 10.30pm, for two nights this happen 1.30, 3am in the morning.
Can I please strongly suggest if a park doesn’t have many vans in don’t park groups right next to each other especially a group of 8-10 17-18 year olds all on red p plates. I get they like to party but they have no respect. Disappointed the park thought this was a good idea.

We booked 3 powered sites for a getaway with friends in our caravans and it was elite! We were close to the beach (could see the water from our vans), beside the amenities block (but at a distance so no smells wafted). Perfect weekend away! We’re so grateful.

Added our dog last minute to the booking without any problems too and she was so relaxed (has never traveled with us either so this built our confidence).
Always a great camping spot. Staff helpful and flexible. Sites and facilities clean

Our unpowered site was a little hard to locate but all good even though other campers were close.
The amenities were clean and looked new. Worth the walk. Near the beach and on a warmer day would have been good for a swim.
The spot was fantastic position and the paddy melons came in on dark as well as bandycouts and a massive native water rat

This is a caravan park which we found to be very good.
The caretaker Dean was extremely friendly and helpful and easy-going. Our site was just what we wanted. The amenities we clean and very good. Don't have to pay for showers. Has a laundry as well. We stayed in the overflow part - so we didn't use the camp kitchen etc, but I'm sure they are great. We were right on the river and the beach. Lots of wallabies around at night too :-)
Love the Port Sorell caravan park. Beautiful location. Excellent amenities and staff are always very friendly and helpful!! Highly recommend to anyone who loves camping whether it’s a tent or a van
Place is spot on to live at close to every thing
We are finding the amenities excellently cleaned and always look tidy.  Thank you

Great experience very friendly and lots to do ☺️
Great for family visits
Great spot for a fishing trip, and nice spacious caravan sites to set-up on.
Excellent value for money. New, modern amenities blocks, easy booking system; thorough, clear communication. Lots of flat, grassy sites with power and water.
Great place. Well used powered site but clean and flat.  New amenities block is great.

We had an unpowered site and our stay was absolutely beautiful. The location is beautiful and the caravan park was well kept and communication from the Caretaker was exceptional. We loved our stay here and will definitely be coming back!
Beautiful location, very clean and well maintained. Owners are friendly and helpful.

Just had 10 days at the Port Sorell Caravan Park. Although the weather was a bit off we had a fabulous time. Dean the caretaker is very helpful and so is his little dog. She is so adorable 🥰. We will certainly be back again !!!
Love our stays at Port Sorell. Close to the beach, plenty of space, with supmarket, cafes nearby. Main facilities modern & clean, and the park crew super friendly & happy to help where they can.

Beautiful location, Clean new facilities, friendly helpful managers.
Thank you site 21
Good park. Excellent communication on booking and check in.
Good Caravan  park kept  in good  order friendly  staff good place  2 fish in estuary l will be coming  back
Highly recommend staying at this huge caravan park. Brand new amenities plus very clean old ones. Huge sites very close to the water. As a bonus, the caretaker Dean is very welcoming and friendly.
Great place to stay

Awesome caretakers who went above their care and help when we had booked the wrong caravan park ( there is NO caravan park in Sorell ) Modern and very clean facilities, with a relaxed feel about the place. Very easy walk to the water. Highly recommended. No stars for rooms...as we stayed on our own motorhome 😆

We have stayed  at the Port Sorell Caravan Park on numerous occasions, and absolutely love it. The staff are amazing and are always going above and beyond to cater for our needs.
It's definitely our favourite place 💜
Great caravan powered site plenty of room. Great amenities and staff excellent.
Poor communication! Totally disappointed 😞

Great van spaces normally a lot of uncontrolled kids screaming at school holiday time but at other times it's perfect ,we dont enjoy the kids playing around the toilet area its an uncontrolled arent thing.
The council are keen to up the fees yet the grounds in the area where we stop have not had any maintenanceexcept for mowing in the 13-14 yrs we have gone there the sites are getting to be a bit bare of grss and not overly ankle friendly,
Caretakers are doing a great job with what they have on the whole ,

I haven't stayed here for a few years but it's had a great transformation. The park was full to capacity & the facilities were cleaned regularly, it's a shame some of the people staying didn't clean up after themselves. Very modern toilet & shower block in the main park area, the paths & walking tracks are endless & cleared & the park in general is tidy & neat.

Great park, very well run,Dean was very helpful,amenities were clean, being able to have fire at night is always a plus.

Sites 203 to 210 could do with a layer of top soil and grass seed.
Great as always,  clean and great management
Lovely clean park. Great amenities. Short stroll to the beach. Fabulous staff.
Our fist time stay here. The park is lovely. The caravan sites were level and spacious. The amenities block was clean and kept tidy. Close to the beaches

Wonderful park. Great location.
Clean and well kept. Hosts were excellent.

Great location huge sites
Friendly welcoming staff..
Great location right on the edge of the beach and walking tracks.
Very relaxing and well maintained park.  A treasure in hiding.  Great amenities and close to the beach
Helpful staff a good place to stay
Had a great time, Dean was very helpful and friendly. Great spot!
Nice quiet caravan park, brilliant communication from the owners. Good friendly service

This holiday park is one of the best we've stayed to date.  It is well off the main road,  so quiet and peaceful,  lots of wildlife.  We stayed in a studio cabin, absolutely perfect,  nice and new and clean, has everything you'd need.  Looked great for the caravans and camper vans that were there too. We just took a walk to the camp kitchen to cook dinner,  it was great,  inside cooking stove and oven,  outside electric BBQ. And a special mention to Josef for being so kind and generous helping us out.  Highly recommend staying here.
A+ highly recommend great place to stay
We have a garden cabin. It is very comfortable with every thing you need for a great stay.

Safari Tent was awesome.
Lots of wildlife, every morning we were met with alot of birds.
Close to everything in the area.

One of the best campsites I have stayed in! We booked it last minute and chose to upgrade to an ensuite site, and it was worth every penny (quite possibly the best upgrade I have ever paid for). The site was level, tidy, and had power and water. The star of the stay was the ensuite: a private bathroom just for us. The shower was hot, and powerful, the bathroom was clean, and it came with clean towels and toiletries. I'd genuinely recommend the ensuite sites to everyone, they were so worth it!
The views were also excellent, and there were pandemelons and a possum which just added to the experience. As well as this, it was nice and close to the Port Arthur Historic Site which was a bonus! They also have a camp kitchen and a pizza oven onsite, as if it couldn't get any better!

Great location
Great for kids
Clean and well equipped kitchen and bathrooms
Really nice camp sites, be aware that many are not flat.
Had a rude encounter with a staff member when checking out an hour late which wasn't a great way to end the weekend.
The expectation of packing up a full tented campsite by 10am with children is very difficult to achieve.

The tourist park is clean, quiet and organised. The location is perfect for walks, beach, enjoying wildlife and access to Port Arthur. We LOVED the Stargazing Safari Tents… even better than the photos!! Hoping to be back!!
park super gut. freundliches personal. auch cool mit den tieren in der nacht
Another great NRMA tourist park. All the facilities you require. Clean and well maintained. Friendly staff. Hard to find fault.
We were first time motorhomers, and this was our first stop. Plenty of room to manouver, decent size sites, we had powered near all facilities. Camp kitchen and toilets were spotless.
Unsafe, filthy, poorly managed, Our kids were harassed in the playground by camping staff

Great level sites for camper. Love the friendly rosellas.
Ask for site up the top if you are unable to walk up to the amenities.
We walked down to the beach and had a lovely swim. Water was so clear. Close to all the sites on the Tasman peninsula.

The location is near to the Port Arthur. But the courter staff service is very average. In the cabin, the setting of the air condition highest temperature is just 25 degrees, so the room is very cold in night time. There is no much food options. Personal option,  it is not worth of staying there. Next time, if I travel again, I would choose stay overnight at Hobart, day trip to Port Arthur.
Bel endroit. Accueil impeccable. Beaucoup d'espace pour chacun. Merci

We spent two nights at a powered en suite site. It was perfect for us. The bathroom was very clean. Nice fresh towels. Hot water. The parakeets and wallabies were a lot of fun for pictures. A bit aggressive tho when cooking or leaving food on the table.
Great site. Highly recommend this place. The en suite bathroom was worth it.
I noticed the glamping tents were nice too but really close to each other. I was happy with our choice.  Stars at night were incredible too.
Love our stay here and highly recommend

We stayed for one night. Campsites are wide and they have fire pits.
Unfortunately not so much sympathy from the lady on reception. Super unfriendly. You can say no to a request nicely with respect or say no with a terrible, unfriendly way to hurt someone that has recently stayed in your campsite. I won’t go back there again.

Really peaceful set up with lots of wildlife and beautiful birds around the campsites.  Toilets, showers and camp kitchen were always found in a very clean state and BBQ areas were well equipped and a cosy fireplace included.  Friendly staff and a great location to explore the natural wonders and historic attractions.
Definitely recommend it as it was not expensive and very convenient to be off grid but have the security of water/dump point and a nice hot shower.

What a crap service.
Very rude staffs.
Sometimes being a human you need to understand the circumstances and be polite to deal with customers.
All the cooking essentials are there :) Close to Port Arthur Historic Site ++
Good spot, near city center.

A spacious and well-equipped campsite offering a wide range of facilities. The grounds are clean and well-maintained, with convenient access to a laundry service. Guests can make use of shared kitchen amenities, including pots, pans, utensils, crockery, and two fridge-freezers.

There’s a pizza oven (which you can ask to setup before 3pm), and plenty of BBQ areas, making it ideal for outdoor cooking and socialising. The campsite also features a large indoor sitting room, along with ample outdoor seating.  The site coded and barricaded.

Located just a 5-minute walk from the beach, guests can enjoy easy access to the shoreline, with a scenic walk leading from the beach to the historic town centre. The site is mainly a dirt and gravel.

My only complaint is the timer on the shower. I understand water saving, but 5 mins is really not enough time to take a shower especially when you need to wash your hair. Once 5mins runs out, you have to wait 5mins for it to re-time again.  Also the WiFi doesn’t work.

Great location close to Port Arthur Historic Site. Clean, well-maintained park with good facilities and easy access for motorhomes. Friendly staff and a really convenient base to explore the area. Would stay again.

This is a beautiful location. We were in one of the Peninsula cabins and it felt modern and comfortable. We did see some ants in the kitchen, however, being right in the woods, I wasn’t surprised. The proximity to the Port Arthur historic site was great and you can even walk there.||The person who checked us in at the reception was not very welcoming. As an example, when I asked her for the Wi-Fi code, she said you’re here for the nature and not to be on your phone. Also, the fragrance smell in the reception area was absolutely overwhelming and gave me an instant headache. The Wi-Fi didn’t work at all our entire four night stay. ||Overall, we enjoyed our stay. There were wallabies about the property, especially at dawn and dusk. There were also beautiful birds flying about and in the fern right outside our window. It was nice that all the windows had screens so we could leave them open overnight. I would return and hope they focus on their customer service.

Trying to book online tells me to contact by phone after 20 mins of waiting line someone picks up to ask to ‘ hold’ how long do you prefer me to wait? Till the next day?
Please get appropiate staffing this is outrageous for the price offered .

Check in lady was rude!
Everything else was fantastic!
The peace and quiet. Other than the rude welcome my only criticism is the water pressure. You need to run around to keep warm

Online booking tells to contact by phone however am being put on hold infinitely
Calling a second time I get hung up immediately without a single word of conversation
Horrible
Tranquilidad y comodidad máximas en plena naturaleza 🍄‍🟫🦘🇦🇺

Rude staff
Upon arrival we were greeted by a lady at reception who came across as rude and condescending, once checked in we headed to our site to find it completely unusable. We had booked for 6 people to be camping in tents, we even called after booking to confirm this could be accommodated, what we got was an approx 4x4m square of hard gravel.
We head back to reception where once again the lady was unhelpful with a response of “we’re booked” when we asked if we could have a different site, thankfully the bloke behind the counter had the opposite approach. He was more than helpful, finding us another site that they kept free for such issues, although he had to swing it past the lady to approve which she did begrudgingly, he even said we could stay an extra night if we’d like as the site was free.

During our stay the park was good, I’d say great if we weren’t woken both mornings by unsupervised kids playing in around our tents in our campsite. Couple out of order ammenities but still enough to go around on a busy weekend.

After a couple good days/nights we decided to leave on our planned date, thinking we could have a slow morning as the site was booked for an extra night, we were almost packed up when around 10.30am someone drove onto our site before getting out to rudely yell “you’re meant to be gone, checkout was 10am and there’s people trying to check in and they can’t because of you” while we can hear the lady from reception carry on over his radio. Funny considering the site was blocked off for us and check in for anyone else wasn’t for another 3.5hrs. We rushed the rest of our pack up, and to top it off the boom gate codes were cancelled so we were stuck in the park (same as the previous morning too).

I doubt we’ll be back, which is a shame as I used to love coming here as a kid.

Joseph is a very decent man – the whole crew is friendly, genuine and always willing to help. From the moment we arrived, they made us feel very welcome.

The park itself is peaceful, comfortable and very well located. It’s close to many beautiful attractions, yet still quiet enough to truly relax. The surrounding area offers so many beautiful places to explore - Cape Raoul, Mt Brown, Historic Site… After a day out hiking or sightseeing, coming back to this calm and relaxing park was always a pleasure.

The only small suggestion would be if the shower time could be a little longer, it would be even better.

Love Claire Jimmy & Jacob

Stayed in the Stargazing Safari tent and it was divine.
Lovely charming space with lots to see out the window. A short 5 minute walk from the beach abd loads of wildlife to see.

Josef the man at the reception desk was clever and sweet and always left a smile on our faces.

We loved our stay here <3

Rude staff
Overall, staying in the family cabin was great until the day we checked out (5 March around 9am). When we tried to exit through the boom gate, we didn’t realise that a password was required.
We asked a staff member (a woman wearing glasses) for help, but she immediately asked, “Where is your paper?” and told me to go find it. I said I might have left it in the cabin, but instead of helping by simply telling us the password, she insisted that I go back to get the paper.
It felt quite rude and not very considerate. A little kindness and understanding would have made the experience much better.

We just spent three nights at the NRMA Port Arthur Holiday park. We are driving around Tassie for three weeks in a small motorhome. We used a powered en suite site here and it was excellent. So clean and stocked with fresh towels and soap.
The layout of the park was so good because we didn’t feel like we were on top of each other with the other campers. So It was a great setting. The camp kitchen and laundry facilities are very good too. All very clean.

There are no views as such, but you are surrounded by beautiful Woodland,wildlife with lots of visits from local Pottaroos and Paddy melons and beautiful birds.
The Reception area is also well stocked with great wine and lots of little necessities and most importantly, fresh coffee.
Would not hesitate to recommend this as a base to explore the area. The owner Joseph was very friendly and helpful and we look forward to coming back.

Stayed at NRMA Port Arthur the last two nights. Incredibly unhappy. $65.25 for an unpowered site that’s uneaven, full of burnt rubbish and overgrown grass. Surrounded by multiple school groups who were feral and had no respect for noise or crossing into other campers sites. Returned back from a night time ghost tour to the school bus parked over 3/4 of our camp site. The bathrooms were absolutely disgusting. Mud all over the floors and showers. Hair all over the sinks and benches. 5 minutes cold showers. Two damaged showers (ladies) and a whole door ripped off in the men’s by school children. While we understand there’s no legal requirement to camp grounds to inform of school camps, putting us in the middle of them is simply not okay.
Only two washing machines available causing a multi hour line up. Additionally only two washing lines which were hogged by the same property for the entirety of our stay.

Lots of wildlife to see and staff were very friendly.

Beautiful site amongst the trees.
Plenty of wildlife and so close to the Historic Site.
Staff are very helpful and friendly. However not so good at replying to emails though but it didn't matter in the end.
Good value for money compared to other Tassie sites.
Very clean place in the nature, nearby the beach.Everything was good.

The waterfront cabins are very comfortable and spotlessly clean. Each cabin has a verandah with a view of the river and at dusk we enjoyed watching the wallabies grazing in the bushes and grasses next to the cabin.
We loved our stay here, it was peaceful & clean. Would 100% come back.

Camped two nights. Had two sunny days but rain on last night. Next to beach/pontoon. Great facilities and helpful/friendly staff. It does share space with public access so can be very busy and noisey at times. Recommended
Beautiful park. The facilities were so clean,amazing. I travel around Australia and was very impressed.
Great showers and wc great location maybe a bit more organised with site parking.  Overall one of the nicest parks in TAS great area to look around & close to both Davenport and Launceston
Friendly helpful staff, beautiful site with excellent facilities. Access to beach was an added bonus!

What a great Caravan Park. Situated right beside a lovely sandy beach & walking/riding paths.
Staff are super helpful from booking to communication and departure.
Facilities are clean, spacious & modern.
Unfortunately the laundry facilities were out of order during our stay which was quite an inconvenience to a lot of people.
In some areas the uneven ground made for uncomfortable tent sites and rickety chairs and tables. I did trip and fall on one occasion which was quite embarrassing.
Overall we had a great time at Port Sorell Caravan Park and highly recommend it.

Great park beside a beautiful beach with concreted walking path.
Dean was very accommodating when I  asked to change sites. Nice clean Amenities and well maintained grassed sites. Will stay again when back in the area
Loved our stay. Highly recommended. We will be back again
One of our favourite places to bring the caravan to.  It has everything we want for a fun filled active but relaxing holiday
Great Park, Thank You Guys
Excellent, proactive communications before & during our stay of 2 nights on a powered van site. Excellent bathrooms (clean, spacious, hot 5 minute showers) & good site & laundry.
Very clean & well maintained facilities. Large sites for RV. Friendly host.

Great spot right near the beach and water so clear and the sites are so large although there is ones with no grass and are boggy and mushy when it rained like ours. The amenities clean and large but need checking went for a shower which are timed thats fine but no need to turn the cold on as the warm water there was had no pressure so it just trickled out of the shower and when hitting the ground made everything wet thank goodness it wasn't cold another plus is 11am check out.

Lovely peaceful park to relax & enjoy your break.
Facilities very clean.
Manager’s very helpful @ pleasant to customers.
A clean well kept park in a beautiful setting. Loved the visits from the wildlife.
Beautiful place to stay

The holiday park is in an excellent location. |We arrived in the early evening after spending the day sightseeing on an easy drive down from Devonport. It is a short drive from there to the Port Arthur historic site, Maingon Bay Lookout & Remarkable Cave , local shops & takeway, and not too far from Hobart. There is a little walk out the front of the tent site that connects you to the beach.|We felt like we were amongst the bushland and we had plenty of wildlife encounters from the moment we arrived - wallabies, potoroos, green rosellas, an echidna, & plenty of crabs. Worth getting the wallaby feed.|The safari glamping tent was a fun novelty for a family with school aged children, but I'm not convinced it was worth it.  Aside from some canvas it was really just a lightweight cabin . The bathroom was fragile & was cold even in summer (thankfully good hot water), there was nowhere to hang damp clothes, it was quite a bit of work making up the bunk beds, & the rustic style furniture was heavy & made access to kitchen items difficult.  We enjoyed sitting on the little verandah with the welcoming flower posy, & the park had a playground for younger kids & there was a laundry room nearby, so for just 2 nights it was fine (even if more expensive than our very comfortable Hobart hotel).
It's a big park lots of accomodation to choose from. Ideally situated to Port Arthur Historical site and all the othe attractions the area has on offer.

We could have done one night here. we booked two. happened to be windy. Dryers are fantastic they actually dry your clothes first
round.
A bit isolated from a pub or restaraunt. easy access to
PArthur historic site which also took us 4 hrs max. tonget around.  We could have moved elsewhere. anyway sat back
and enjoyed the breeze.
熱愛野生動物的我們非常喜歡這裡的環境，黃昏有許多小動物出沒，也可以步行至附近的海邊很漂亮；唯一可惜的是帳篷的體驗感遠大於舒適，雖然帳篷很新奇，但房內暖爐不夠力、只有客廳才溫暖、在廁所及床的地方晚上會很冷，且半夜風很大、帳篷會被吹的一直發出很大的聲響、完全無法入睡；有機會下次會再來，但是會選擇一般廂房。

We stayed in a glamping tent. It didn't live up to our expectations, and would have been unbearable with kids in tow.

The main issue is the limited space in the tent, and the poor effort that has gone into selecting furnishings. The main living area has three main areas - a living space, a bedroom, and a bathroom. There is also a balcony with BBQ, which we didn't use due to the weather.

The living space was filled with a cabinet on one side which contained some crockery etc, a small fridge and the tiniest television set up I have seen in years. The batteries in the remote didn't work, so we had to swap them around to get things going. You have to use a set top box to watch TV, just like in the 1990s. The cabinet has a sliding door, so to access some things you have to slide the door over others. It's just frustrating. The only seating inside is a single couch with a couple of very small coffee tables. With any more than one small child you're going to be forced to head outside. There are no curtains for the front of the cabin, which is one big glass door. Kids were roaming the park in the evening.

The bedroom area is separated from the living space by a tethered double flap canvas curtain. If it attached in the middle we didn't see how, so there was a small permanent gap. One side had a bunk bed, the top bunk was close to the roof of the tent. On the other side was a double bed. This was pushed up against the wall, and with the couch in the living area at the end of the bed there was access to the bed only from one side, making access and egress clumsy.

The bathroom area was the only section with solid walls (no roof, just walls), but these sat inside the canvas walls and roof. Ours came with an assortment of wildlife from moths in the sink to a spider living in the soap holder.

We found the park difficult to communicate with, compounded by their telephone system being down.

Overall, we found this stay disappointing and poor value for money. The glamping tents aren't fit for more than two people, and the way in which they have tried to fit in a family means that the limited space is poorly used. Maybe their cabins are better.

We stayed here 2 nights. 1 night in a waterfront cabin and the other night in Glamping Tent.
The cabin was run down with gaps in the flooring and no water pressure but had a lovely view past the other cabins.
The tent was much smaller with no kitchen but outdoor bbq and sink but large bathroom! Only issue with the tent was the "flaps" for the bedroom didnt close completely as there no blind on the main door.

Uncompromising, but polite about it.
Felt like dealing with someone pedantic, inflexible and maybe even greedy... But with a smile!
No option to reschedule, no option to hold onto credit, no understanding, even after giving plenty of notice.
Disappointing and unpleasant experience overall.
Not recommended.

It was good with the exception of the WiFi. They have WiFi but it’s only at the office and can’t be used from sites. No mention on that at booking or checkin. Don’t advertise something if you effectively don’t have it.

$38 per night for an unpowered site. In return you get an uneven, not well maintained but dirty campsite full of garbage. Facilities are ok, but need more regular cleaning.
Overall, not worth the money!

We have just had two nights at this park. The staff were so accomodating and went out of their way to help us when we needed an extra night.
Unfortunately we were not able to get any more nights at this beautiful spot and I know why. It’s one of the nicest we’ve ever stayed in!
Thanks again.
Mark and Jo Flew

Went with the family (2 adults & 2 kids). Amenities are good for the little ones. Lots of space for kids to to play and enjoy the day, ride their bikes anywhere including a bike pump track, playground and even a small bouncy castle. WE had a blast. our sport was a non-powered site for 3 days and it was so good to be close to the bathrooms, kitchen and common areas. Facilities are clean and well maintained.  One suggestion I give you, is to plan your trip and book in advance those prime spots. Download your caravan park map and book your spot. Reception will accommodate your needs in advance, and they even sell ice and ice-cream for the young ones. Amazing family-oriented location to enjoy with the family making BBQ's, cooking marshmallows on your fire while having a nice relaxing beverage. You can also do pizza (just go to reception and book your time in advance when you arrive to the park). Prime location close to boat ramps, the Penitentiary, Remarkable cave, safety cove and many other incredible spots. Wildlife is abundant everywhere, and if you are lucky, beautiful Rosella parrots will come close to you, looking for seeds.
地點偏遠，其餘可以
Excellent quality park. Stayed in a powered site arriving late. Service was exceptional, park facilities were best on our 10 day trip.

This site is great and we really loved our stay here. In the evening, birds such as crows and parrots flew around our campervan looking for food, which was such a fun experience.

Make sure to buy the bird and wallaby food at the reception before they close, as you’ll definitely use it. The parrots especially loved it. We enjoyed feeding and interacting with the parrots, and later on, the wallabies came close to us as well.

The facilities were clean, and the reception staff were friendly and helpful. Overall, it was a lovely and memorable stay!
Fire wood delivered to your site.

Toilet and bathroom facility are not comfortable to use. Not very clean and not well kept. The kitchen and laundry area are acceptable although not very clean. Admin should prepare toilet cleaning tools for users to clean after for the next users if they are not up to the job. Same wise for the kitchen, kitchen towels should be given to campers to help clean up the kitchen. Reception does sell some food and essential items.
A spotlessly clean and comfortable cabin. Would definitely stay again.
A wonderful place to stay, cooling and peaceful.

We stayed two nights at the Peninsula cabins in the NRMA Holiday Park in Port Arthur and found the cabins were very comfortable.  Clean, modern, spacious and the owners obviously care a great deal about their park.  Everything within the park complex was tidy. The childrens play area well maintained.  ||Gated entry for vehicles, cabins with gates to prevent local wildlife entering and soiling the deck areas.||Provision of individual barbecues on the deck, a lovely touch - limiting oil splatter and smells within the kitchen lounge dining area.  The spa bath was a lovely bonus after a full day exploring Port Arthur.||Owner on checking in, very welcoming.||Would recommend and stay again.  Even the luxury tent accommodation looked fantastic.

The waterview cabin we stayed in was great! Everything was as expected.Lots of wildlife in the park, and the receptionist was nice enough to give our child a free pack of bird feed. Had a blast.Highly recommend.

Great. Loved the place and the service.
We were on a caravan
Amazing natural grounds, with beautiful trees and water views. Bathroom facilities are quite tired and need refreshing.
Best equipped kitchen in a holiday park I have seen
The people were lovely birds Wallabies were friendly room were   brilliant had the best experience wish we could stay for two nights BEST MOTEL YET!!!

On check in staff couldn't find my booking even though was emailed the day before to check in on line, staff member wanted me to go back to car to prove my booking, showed evidence and was asked what sites number did I have, told her she should tell me, gave me a site number and told me she was only doing her job. On looking at email our sites was given to someone else. The last 14 nights have stayed at a lot of parks and NRMA was the worse won't be going back.
Nice facilities, staff and prices. Helped reunite me with a lost item too. Thank you!
Loved this place. The wild life was so friendly and beautiful beach

Well equipped tent. Nice location. Very near port arthur historic site. You can even walk to the site in 3o min. Nice walk. Very lovely terrace with view on Stewart Bay. Lots of wallabies around the tent. Heating system in the tent.
Clean and plentiful facilities, very close to Port Arthur which is handy when you want to do late activities as the wildlife will be more abundant from dusk to dawn.
Great place and great service. It’s in the middle of the woods so was really quiet and peaceful. Room had everything we needed. We really enjoyed and hope to visit again
Nice park. Well kept. We had a wheel chair cabin although we didn't need it and we much appreciated the extra space.

We stayed in the Stargazing Safari Tent for one night while visiting Port Arthur and the Tasman National Park. My husband and I both agree that this is one of the coolest accommodations we have stayed at. We both enjoyed a nice soak in the bathtub on the patio and even saw some larakeets. Had a small kitchenette inside and a grill on the patio as well! Wish we could have stayed there longer.
Very good environment. Value to stay.

Cabin has everything, kitchen, 2 bedrooms, and 1 double decker bed, very good.   Beds were comfortable, toiletries provided was sufficient.  We feeder the wallabies, possums and bird’s outside our cabin from 6 pm onwards.   We also walk to sea side from the camping site.  There should more activities  can do in the camping site if you stay more than  1 day. Satisfied with the stay.

Cabin 20 with water views. Awesome.
Peaceful, comfortable and wildlife around in the afternoon. Close to all the attractions. Great value

Perfect location, lovely cabin that suited our family well. Had all the amenities needed and kids loved the playground. Short walk to the beach and bushland surrounding. Staff lovely and very helpful. Would highly recommend.

Beautiful caravan park in an amazing location, however our site was left a little dirty by previous campers and we got a small rubbish bag full while cleaning up. Will still return. Great place to camp with a dogs.

Dump point was over flowing rubbish skips overflowing rubbish on the ground maggots and the camp spots were all dirt no grass needs serious maintenance level out the big holes in the ground I rolled my ankle on one of the large holes. We pay over $40 a night so we expect the grounds to be clean and safe like other caravan parks
Very welcoming, friendly and helpful staff and a beautiful place to stay

Well appointed caravan park in a great location. Large sites and pet friendly. Great pizza place just outside the park within walking distance and other shops only a short drive away. New amenities block and well equipped camp kitchen.
Have been camping here for 10 years. Love every minute of it. We “tent” camp. Nothing like going for a walk on the beach, when it’s on your door step
Fantastic communication, very clean facilities great experience

This really is one of Tassie's little gems in terms of a caravan park. Everything was as it should be, with friendly and welcoming staff, excellent facilities and a calm environment with families and couples enjoying the holiday. So close to the beach for a dip, and an enjoyable 10min walk to the vibrant shops, cafes and Country Club at Shearwater.

Highly recommend Port Sorell Caravan park, extremely clean and tidy, amenities were spotless. Staff were amazing and nothing was a bother. We wished we had booked a longer stay. Will definitely be back and will certainly book longer next time. For such a busy park it was surprisingly very quiet every night. Well done team 🙌🏻🙌🏻

We really enjoyed our yearly camp this year, made even better by the gorgeous weather we had for most of the week that we were there.  If there was one thing that would make the stay even better, would be free wifi.  Having travelled extensively on mainland Australia - unlimited free wifi for the cost of the site, should be provided.  In saying that, it is good to switch off  and chill out
Great place to stay with family right on the beach park facilties all very clean and in good condition
Fantastic place to stay. Beach extremely close by. Amenities always clean and tidy. Would highly recommend
We had an unpowered caravan site right next to the beach, bathrooms and toilets were always stocked and super clean! Would definitely camp here again!
Just visiting but looked very nice, crowded with families for holidays. Enjoying themselves.
First time at the park. It is fantastic, we will definitely be back. Facilities are clean, showers were great, overall a great experience

It was a site in a beautiful setting, easy access to a glorious beach walk, great amenities and the staff were friendly and informative.  We have no hesitation in recommending this NRMA site to anyone, we were in a motorhome.

We stayed overnight in a star gazing glamping tent. Our children loved sleeping in the cosy nook of two single beds. Every amenity was provided and the fireplace + air con kept us warm the whole night. Beds were comfy and we stayed in for dinner using the bbq on our deck - win-win after a long day exploring the historic Port Arthur settlement. A walk after dinner around the site gave us lots of potoroos to see on dusk, and our tent had a beautiful view of the Port Arthur bays. I'm not sure how you star gaze though, as our deck was completely covered with a canvas awning and we had to go outside in order to see the sky/stars. A great stay though, we loved it.
Overnight stay at NRMA Port Arthur Holiday Park in a Studio Cabin. It was a bit on the small side (especially the bathroom) but it was clean, warm, modern and nicely furnished. WiFi was good too.
Overnight stay at NRMA Port Arthur Holiday Park in a Studio Cabin. It was a bit on the small side (especially the bathroom) but it was clean, warm, modern and nicely furnished. WiFi was good too.
Enjoyed the stay, and feeding the wallabies and  birds,(food available at the office) close to Port Arthur
Lovely bush setting, had ensuite site with fire pit. Ensuite clean and well equipped and fantastic communal facilities. Local wildlife a treat
Loved our stay here. The tents could do with a spring dusting, but we loved the ambience and location as well as the facilities on site
Great facilities and friendly staff

I stayed here in the bunk beds and after a day out and about as I returned tied.Only for the house keepers to throw out my food and drinks.Good location close to walking tracks and attractions.But the service needs to be better.

I did not have a good experience here. We stayed two nights. I slept on the bottom bunk of a bunk bed, the bed itself was more comfortable than I anticipated but the but creaked like mad whenever I moved.

The biggest issues were those manageable by Park Management. When we checked in there were some major issues. There are sliding doors in the cabin,  before checked in they hadn't been checked and were off their rails. Not easily movable, we had to physically lift them until I figured out what was going on. The other was unclean dishes, the bowls in particular. Small but on top of everything else I was just frustrated.

This was my 4th park in 10 days and by far the worst experience.

A very lovely place to stay, the staff are friendly and have lots of info. The park itself is quite large and clean with the best camp kitchen I have ever seen at a camp site. Well worth staying here!
Was ok but nothing flash. Clean and tidy

It was a shame the weather was wet during our stay, but it didn't stop us from having a good time. The glamping tents at NRMA Port Arthur Holiday Park were spot on—cosy and warm, which was perfect for the miserable weather. Our five-year-old loved seeing the birds wandering by, and we even spotted a possum near our barbecue. We were also super close to the Port Arthur Historic Site, which meant we could still get out and explore between the showers. The glamping tents are a great option for families and we'd definitely recommend them.
Quiet and peaceful park with a short walk to the beach. There are many types of cabins to choose from, we stayed in the waterview cabins and they were quite nice.

Beautiful park in a bush setting overlooking the bay. The van sites were very spacious and had their own fire pits. It was lovely to see and feed the wildlife. Great park, would definately stay again.
Wildlife and friendly services, great spot. Even saw a local echidna we had a powered site.

Have just spent 3 weeks at Moomba staying with friends. It is a really great park, very friendly and accomodating. Vicki and Mick make you feel very welcome
Thankyou, Wendy
Very well setup, and neat and tidy park.
Great spot, we booked a ensuite site, nice stay,

great caravan park nice bbq area and laundry mat with washing machine and dryer which are coin operated showers and toilets we did not use showers as we booked a en-suite site which had toilet and shower but I did see a sign saying $1.00 for 3 minutes shower at main shower block
It was very quiet which we needed and having an on suite was amazing well worth the money close and handy to everything as well ,the owners were very welcoming xx
We stayed two nights and we loved it. Vicky is great, very friendly and helpful 👍

A lovely quiet place, great location it suited to all our needs, our children enjoyed it they kept themselves busy cosy comfortable accommodation very clean, thank you to mick and Vicky for your hospitality at your holiday park we will most defiantly be returning for future stays

Our family of 4 stayed for 3 nights. It was so cute and homely! Very clean, close to all the cool stuff and the park owners are beautiful people who truly care about their guests stay! Highly recommend this park, 5 stars!

Stayed two nights at Moomba Holiday and Caravan Park. Cabin had everything we needed. Comfortable, clean and a quiet area. The kids loved the awesome park near by with bike tracks/jumps, bbq area, basketball court. Within walking distance to a gorgeous beach! A lovely feel about the place and the kids are already asking to go back!

We stayed here for one night. The bedroom was small, no room to move. No conditioner or shampoo, lucky I took my own. The bathroom was filthy, nowhere to put the soap etc. The water trickled out in the shower. The lounge room was very small, the TV was on top of us. Location was good.
So close to the beach and golf course
We really  enjoyed  our stay  here ,nice quiet  area

Great stay. Helpful new park people Dean and Steve. Great communication. Amenities so much cleaner.
Hopefully able to keep park costs down

Love camping at Port Sorrell, Amenities are good and location is great also. Only complaint is think they need to spend a bit more on the ground as our spot is just dirty sand, dirt. Could sow some grass seed over the off season and keep a bit of water on it.
Great location for camping, especially for families. Highly recommended as a place to stay.

This caravan park is amazing great location, easy access, clean showers and toilets plus showers are free! Owners happy to help and very friendly, Kids under 18 free and for a powered site it was a great price will definitely be making more trips to Port sorell
Great caravan park for families with excellent facilities and amenities to use especially for longer stays. This was our second year of staying and we will be regulars from now on.
Excellent in all aspects

Located in an iconic area with excellent m/home sites.
Amenities are always kept clean and tidy.
Was a great place to stay at and other campers where so friendly.

The sites had little to no grass and were mainly sand and tent pegs just pulled out including screw sand pegs. The site needs to be rested and reseeded.. the showers were great and the management were friendly.
I had an unpowered tent site. Lovely location, good, clean facilities. Would recommend.

Great spot, great for kids with the flat concrete path and nearby playgrounds.
Communication and reliability from Dean has been great :)
Great couple of weeks and amenities were clean and done every day. Great park to stay at.

The caravan park was in a good location and the amenities were clean and well maintained. We had to change sites for our second night as they were ‘apparently’ fully booked but the site we moved to on day 2 hadn’t been used at all the day before. It would have been great if they could have taken the time to go and have a look - would have saved us a lot of hassle and although we did ask numerous times before check in, they weren’t interested in helping.
We had another terrific stay at the caravan park. Very happy with the location, amenities and services. Thanks!

We love Port Sorell Caravan Park and have been annually visiting the campsite since 2002 with our children - now adults.  We've stayed on mostly powered sites around the park over the years.  My only encouragement (in lieu of complaining) would be to sow some grass on sites.  Some sites have grassed areas but ours this year was very dusty and basically dirt.  We look forward to greener pastures next year : )

We took our caravan to this park, there was ample room for additional swags at our site, the amenities where spotless, from what I saw cleaned at least twice daily.  The camp kitchen was great, super markets and take away close by. I highly recommend a stay here and can't thank the care takers enough for their continued patience with me as I had to change my booking due to illness.  All in all our stay was wonderful and we will be back again and again!
Awesome spot

Best place to stay in Port Arthur... the location is great. The service and the staff are what makes it special. Linda and Janet at the reception desk go the extra mile to help you out and are willing to go out of their way to make any changes that will improve your stay. I highly recommend. Firepits and even fire 'rooms' are available, so you can roast marshmallows even if it is raining.

Great location in close proximity to Port Arthur Historic site. $2 kids bags for native bird seed and wallaby food made for some close encounters. The pizza oven was a hit with Eric rolling the dough with a local bottle of Pinot Noir.

Calling all nature lovers! This park has abundant wildlife with an array of amenities for people of all ages. We stayed at two powered sites and were greeted by pademelons and possums all night long. Park management also provide wood fires in two common areas to while away the evenings. Nice walk along Stewart’s trail to the Port Arthur Historic site. We, and other guests, encountered ongoing problems with codes for the security gate at the entrance of the park. Buttons aren’t clear and at one stage we had to wait 30 mins due to a malfunction. Bathrooms a little dated, but still acceptable. Good value for members of motor clubs. The friendly staff gave us some great local tips. Highly recommend!
We stayed in a studio cabin that is most affordable. This is the best park we've ever stayed at. The design and layout is excellent and caters for all types of travellers and vehicles.

3 nights in star gazer tent. Lovely view and plenty of wildlife. Clean, great features and was cosy and warm despite the weather (was unsure of booking a 'tent' at this time of year). Kids enjoyed the playground, pump track and DVD library. Stewart's Bay is stunning and only 5-10 minute walk. Staff were very friendly.

A camping experience that doesn't feel crowded.  The wild life and birds are something else . The facilities are clean and well maintained. The camp caters for every budget from unpowered to lux cabins. Definitely 1 of our favourites.

Terrible customer service. Lady/manager at front reception was awfully rude, for no apparent reason, not only to us but also to her staff. Made us feel very unwelcome. We thought maybe she was just having a bad day, but her behaviour was the same at check in and check out. The remaining staff were kind, however. Beautiful location and ok amenities, but definitely would not recommend staying here due to the terrible customer service.

RUDE RECEPTIONIST!

This is a lovely place, and overall we had a great stay with a wonderful experience. However, at check-out, things went very wrong. We were a little late (around 30 minutes), and the gate didn’t open, so we asked the receptionist for help. Instead of handling the situation professionally, the lady at the front desk (wearing glasses) was rude and humiliating. I even felt discriminated against by the way she confronted me.
If she had spoken politely and explained the issue, I would have happily paid the extra charge for overstaying. But the way she treated me completely ruined the end of our trip. Because of this, I would not want to come back here if the same receptionist continues serving guests.
Hospitality should always make guests feel respected, even when problems arise. Sadly, this was not the case.

Best campsite I have ever stayed.
Very competitive price, only ~60 usd per night for a small room with private washroom.
Very tidy, new and clean. With basic kitchen ultensils.
A very peaceful environment with lots of wildlife around.

Great location, atmosphere and facilities at the Holiday Park. We were a group of 30. Accommodation was clean and comfortable. The shared kitchen area is well maintained and the staff are friendly and helpful.
Beautiful location. Very comfortable cabins. Very clean and comfy beds

I came here with a large group booking and the stay exceeded expectations. I think its the most beautiful location and holiday park I have ever stayed. Thank you for being so accommodating and the pizza oven was a hit! I highly recommend this place to anyone.
Good
Great place to stay in Port Arthur and only 5 minutes drive to visit Port Arthur itself. Stayed in a studio room which was clean and comfortable.

We were only here for 2 nights  but we found the park ideally situated a short walk from a lovely beach and only a short distance from the main local attractions .Our cabin was very nice inside and well finished.

Pre booked 4 night stay, ensuite site, 2 adults. The park is set in a natural bush setting, lots of well proportioned terraced sites and cabins. Boom gates at entry and exit require code. Wildlife and birds are abundant. Laundry is coin operated, 4 dryers but only 2 washing machines.

It lacked maintenance, peeling paint, delaminated door, dead insects and cobwebs and a rusting light fitting. There was no grab handle for the steps and the door opened outward. Downpipe was broken, solar/sensor light directed to the steps didn’t work. Exceptionally disappointing given it is a branded park.
Its only saving grace for us was the proximity to the attractions and tours of Port Arthur.

If you’re staying in a tent, this campground might be a nightmare for you. The area is home to many wild animals, and the unpowered sites feel more like decorative afterthoughts than properly maintained camping areas. The grounds are full of animal droppings, rocks, overgrown weeds, and uneven terrain — it was impossible to sleep without rolling around all night.

At night, wild animals would come right up to the tent, and even go inside while we were sleeping, making a mess and stealing food (sigh). To make things worse, some people who weren’t even camping in tents would come out just to play with and feed these wild animals.

There were no signs in the area warning people not to feed wildlife. The amenities were placed in very inconvenient locations — if you’re staying at the far end of the unpowered sites, you’ll have to walk a long way just to use them.

And the showers? A 5-minute time limit with barely any water pressure. At first, when there weren’t many people, you could tell the facilities were being cleaned. But later on, it was obvious that maintenance had stopped — the toilets were consistently filthy.

The laundry facilities were also inadequate — only two washing machines and four dryers for the entire campground, with one of the dryer appeared to be fully functioning with no warning or error signs, yet it didn’t produce any heat. We unknowingly wasted our money on it, like tossing coins into the ocean.

This is by far the most disappointing campground I’ve stayed at. Even though the location is decent, unless you’re staying in a building or a campervan, it’s going to be a disaster.

Wunderschöner Campingplatz mit allem was das Herz begehrt! 👍🙌
Viele Tiere am Abend und tolle Lage!

We fell in love with our Peninsula cabin at this well maintained holiday park. It was perfect for us as we had the accessible access cabin with a ramp and no stairs. It also had a fabulous big safety bathroom. We had a balcony with a great little bbq grill. We cooked both nights. Just outside our cabin at dusk there were little wallabies. Also lots of birds including gorgeous green rosellas. There was a wonderful bush track down to Stewart's Bay beach. We loved the Tasman Peninsula for all the natural features and the historical Port Arthur.  Book here!
Very friendly staffs , room is very nice with balcony overlooking water.
Definitely a great place to stay, great facilities and clean

Recently stayed 2 nights on our trip around Tassie. Loved the bush setting and the wildlife that came to visit. Quiet and peaceful setting. Amenities were clean and tidy. Great camp kitchen and BBQ/fireplace (for cold nights) amenities provided. Close to Port Arthur historic site and much more. Staff really friendly and helpful. Would definitely recommend staying here.

Fantastic spot..each camp-site had its own fire pit and ensuit..beautiful surroundings with tonnes of wild life..we had poteroos,kookaburras and parrots all coming to the camp site..plus it's the only camping ground that we've been to so far that allows fire pits so that's def a added bonus..can def understand the other ones we stayed at not allowing it as they were in national parks ect so that's makes this park just that bit more special in my book...close to the historical site and great value...100% def stay a night or 2 here..you won't regret it..

Great service, friendly and welcome after hours. The place secure. The care taker look after me and advised best interests place to visit like chocolate factory, cheese factory, raspberry farm lunch and ice cream store . He is superb one
I highly recommended and will be back
Thanks again

Our experience at Port Sorell Caravan Park was absolutely fantastic! (Minus Tassie crazy weather) but that did not disappoint From the moment we arrived, we were greeted with genuine warmth and hospitality that instantly made us feel at home.

The park itself is beautifully kept  clean, peaceful, and perfectly located for exploring the stunning North west of Tasmania.

A special mention has to go to Dean, the caretaker — he is truly outstanding! Dean goes above and beyond for every guest, making sure everything runs smoothly and everyone feels welcomed.

His friendly nature, attention to detail, and willingness to help with anything you need make all the difference. It’s rare to come across someone who takes such pride in their role and genuinely cares about guests’ experiences ,  Dean does exactly that!

If you’re looking for a comfortable, well-maintained, and friendly place to stay, don’t look any further than Port Sorell Caravan Park. We’ll definitely be back — and it’s all thanks to the incredible service and atmosphere Dean creates.

We enjoyed our stay,weather was  a bit rough but that's okay, we are  disappointed about the camp fires that are burning on the ground it destroys the root structure in the grass and takes a long time to recover, we make a point of having our fire in a elevated fire pot which has no affect on the grass, we think it should be mandatory where no elevated fire pot, no fire ,not a big ask the grass in the camping area is gold, we have been every year for the past 30 years,the area is looking better each year thanks to the grass covering the sand cheers Karen and Leigh Lockwood.

Great spot
Upgraded park facilities
Friendly staff
Great communications
Great caravan park.  Fantastic facilities and location

Such an amazing Caravan Park. Well set out, sparkling clean amenities and grounds and situated in such a beautiful spot. Quick access to the beach, a Woolies nearby and great places to eat and explore. We will definitely be returning. The Service and communication before we arrived was spot on and appreciated. Fantastic customer service.

A beautiful location, the guys running it were very friendly and professional.
The only thing that needs improvement is the toilet block at the end of the park. It needs a good clean (mould everywhere) or a paint. But to be honest I have seen worse.

Great facilities , nice location. Don’t stay near the car park  near the jetty, you will be woken up by cars doing burnouts in the middle of the night. Mite be s good idea for council to lock that carpark off at certain times .
Great park, clean well maintained, bookings and check out seamless, can’t recommend high enough 👍
Wish we found this earlier than our last night on Tassie. Great spot right on the beach. Given our September stay, the park was only 5%full so lovely and quiet.

We always have a wonderful experience staying at Port Sorell Caravan Park. The team here are more than accommodating with bookings, making the whole process easy and stress-free. The amenities are always spotless and well maintained, which made our stay so comfortable. On top of that, the service is consistently warm and welcoming — you really feel looked after. We continue to book here as we absolutely love it, and we couldn’t recommend this park more highly!
Very tidy and clean park highly recommended great amenities

We were close to the beach, fished off the pontoon and rode our bikes on the concrete tracks around the town.
Amenities are fantastic, the showers are brilliant with enough room to dress without  being a contortionist.
We liked the water saving measures taken in the park, well done all.

Lovely camp site ,excellent shower
Lots of native animals
Friendly staff
Great spot
Was a lovely stay.  Dean the caretaker is a very nice knowledgeable fellow.  The only grip I have was with the timed showers they weren’t hot enough. After all it is winter.

Great caravan park, we had the park to ourselves where we picked to set up, close to amenities and track to the beach. We were close to the public carpark but wasn't an issue. Plenty of wildlife when the sun goes down and birds chirping and visiting the site throughout the day. Thai food van was worth checking out, food was fresh and delicious. Would definately visit again. Happy campers.

Port Sorell Caravan Park is one of my favourite parks to stay at. It is always clean and well maintained. The staff are super friendly and always accommodating. The location is perfect as there is a great walking/bike track along the waterfront. Gorgeous beaches
just a stone's throw from camp sites. Recommend for families, in fact any travellers 👌
Good park and facilities. Not large, 99 total accommodation, camping and caravan sites. Roadways are a bit narrow  for large car and caravan combinations.

Best Holiday Park I have been to thus far. The rooms were very nice especially for that price I was impressed. I didn't want to camp because of the weather I just needed a roof over my head. Good value for money.
Very friendly staff, great bush camp with  camp fires and lovely big pitches.

We stayed here in our van. The office ladies were lovely. The park is so beautiful with a lovely short walk to a beautiful bay.The amenities are clean. Port Arthur convict site is just down the road. TC
Great spot. Ideal if you're visiting Port Arthur.
Amazing place to stay

We stayed at their Studio Cabin for 4 days and 3 nights. We think the location is ideal for hiking the 3 Capes Tracking Routes and returning daily. Additionally, it's about a 15-minute drive to Maingon Bay Lookout for aurora hunting. If we ever return to visit Tasman National Park, we'll choose this accommodation again.

Excellent garden cabin. well equipped.room i warm and bed has a warmer too!Linda and the other guy at reception (didnt catch his name) are super warming and helpful.spotted wallabies and birds right outside cabin as well

We only started for a night, but if we had more time we would have stayed longer.
Friendly service, fantastic amenities and the wildlife around the campground was a bonus (and a delight to our 2.5 year old). Great value for money

I stayed in a Stargazing Glamping Tent and I was thrilled with the accommodation, the small touches for arrival and general inclusions/value. It was just like the photos!

The service from the front desk was lovely on both occasions.

It's extremely close to the Historic Site and close to the (limited) surrounding food options.

Look forward to visiting again!

離亞瑟港歷史景點很近
