site有火盆 有很多小袋鼠在四處跳
It was spotless, amazing place, naturally beautiful, lots of potaroos and other animals grazing around, very nice facilities for cooking, clean toilets... all in all a fantastic place.

Drove in here without booking ahead as it was the off season, mid August. The staff were really friendly and helpful, we were going to stay 1 night but went back and booked a second night after seeing all the furry and feathery friends that were hanging around the campervan. Plenty of accommodation options from tents to cabins. Our site had power, water, grey water drain and we also splashed out and got an ensuite site with its own lockable toilet/shower room complete with towels and soaps. This place has everything from laundry to a BMX track. We also like the walking path to the port arthur historical site which meander through forests and beaches, just an easy 30 min walk one way. Good free wifi and we'll maintained grounds.

Great customer service.
Friendly & welcoming…
Fair price….we paid $48 for an ensuite site for the night.
Would stay again if we are in the area.

Second time we’ve stayed here. Staff are lovely. Park really well maintained. Stayed in a water view cabin this time. Rooms are super clean with everything you need. Really well located and great prices. Short walk to Stuart’s Bay beach from the campground is a great way to start the day. Plenty of wildlife for the kids and specific food available from reception for feeding them. The communal toilets and camp kitchen are really well maintained. The possums are a bit of a nightmare as they like to come right up on to the balcony or scavenge at the bins for food.

Location was amazing, near to almost everything around Port Arthur!

Room is very subpar though, the aircon does not work consistently, the blanket is very uncomfortable and the worst part is that the hot water runs out very quickly! we were a couple travelling and we were not able to shower back to back🫥

What a spot!!! Tassie local and I will be staying here more regularly now that I know it exists. We stayed in the star gazing tents. Amazing value for money. The kids absolutely loved it. The quality of the accommodation was impressive.

This is the best kept secret in Tasmania! We stayed in a Star Gazing Safari Tent with our two kids in the middle of winter. It was so warm, with two heating options, impressively spacious and the beds were so comfy. There was a full size fridge, and fully stocked kitchenette, and a Weber BBQ for cooking. The amount of space where you could sit and relax was truly remarkable. We could sit outside and still be fully undercover and protected from wind and rain. The kids loved their single beds inside a hidden cubby. The TV had all the options of watching Netflix, ABC iview, even YouTube and Disney+. The bathroom was so clean and new, with a full size rain head shower and outdoor bath. This was easily the best place to stay at Port Arthur, walking track access and 5 mins to the beach, 1 min drive to the boat ramp and 3 mins to the General Store. The staff were super friendly and do an amazing job keeping the park clean and tidy. Really loved out stay 5 stars.

Caravan park has an amazing fireplace in the camp kitchen area with firewood available free of charge. Huge camp kitchen with everything on offer amazing walking track down to the water. Woodfired pizza oven available to everyone to use free of charge, just need to book it in with staff first who will light it for you in readiness to use. Extremely spacious caravan park. Best ever!
Amazing experience

Port Arthur has a special place in my heart, this place definitely contributed to that.
We stayed in the ‘Stargazing Tent’. Was incredible. Loved every minute of it. The tent (which is honestly just a canvas cabin. Was amazing!) was beautiful and cozy.
Our favourite part of the park is the wildlife. You’re really in the trees and enjoying the views. There is a sneaky little path down to a beach which is beautiful to look at. Would definitely recommend staying here with any amount of people. Adults, kids and everyone in between will love this place.

Amazing park in a spectacular location. We stayed in a waterfront cabin which had everything we needed and a beautiful view of the water. We loved being surrounded by the local wildlife. A short walk takes you to a little quiet beach, and the amenities in the park are great and very well-kept.
房中無WiFi,只可接待處收數據，但只有1G限量。騙局。與Agoda 描述有出入。非常失望。接待人員更理所當然。

Beautiful location and accommodation, and excellent value for money. Stayed in studio cabin for 2, clearly nearly new, very comfortable and cosy. The park is in a spectacular location, immediately adjacent to beautiful Stewart's Bay, provides lots of space around cabins and caravan/camping sites, and is a very short distance from the Port Arthur Historic Site, and local dining options. Michelle at reception was very friendly and helpful. Overall, one of the best parks we've stayed at, and will definitely stay again.
We stayed at a powered unsuited site and loved it the wild life was amazingly friendly our children really enjoyed the local green Rosella's that where landing on them and eating out of there hands.
It was alright but the wifi was not working half the time

Place is close to everything worth visiting at Port Arthur. Very comfortable place in nature. Cottage is clean with full amenities. Toasty even on a chilly day or night. Always a treat to “go home” after hike, walks and exploring around. Grocery is just around the corner, tasman cruise loading jetty also a mere 3 minutes drive, historic site etc etc etc are all very close. You are in the center of it all so turn left or right and they are really just 5-10minutes drive away. Will definitely return to this place. Highly recommended!
非常好的住宿，免費泊車，車到門口，晚上有很多星星，有野生袋鼠非常可愛，前台為我們免費升級了房間，十分豪華
Stayed in peninsula cabin. Really clean and very nice facilities, amazing value. Friendly staff and great spot.

This place deserves 15/10. The staff were lovely, nothing was too much trouble. The park was so clean, and amenities were great. We stayed in a stargazer safari tent, and I could not fault it. I would live there. So cosy, even on a rainy miserable night.
So wonderfully maintained park, staff so friendly and helpful, stayed in a stargazer safari tent absolutely immaculate.
Well maintained site friendly staff

Great service from staff, excellent price & amenities.
Surrounded by Beautiful wildlife .
Sit by the fire & the night comes to life.

Plenty of space for caravans, easy access to the beach.
Couple of minor issues, people with dogs off lead against park rules, we had a few loose dogs approach ours and the 6am Sunday skip bin collection is the reason for only 4 stars.
Lovely place to stay, very clean toliet and showers, so close to the beach for walks , and even saw wildlife, will be back again
Very friendly welcome . Great location.
Awesome friendly service in an amazing caravan park on the beach. Everything is on offer ..... walking tracks, reserves, bike riding, kayaking, fishing and of course cafes ☕️
a great park , good facilities ,plenty of sites and right on the beach , its on our return visit list.
Absolutely beautiful place ❤️😎

The reception agent was extremely helpful, Dean gave us many hints and tips even gave us a very useful website to use whilst we visit Tasmania.
Thank you Dean

We had a plug in site right near the beach and across from facilities, so very peaceful with Paddimelons and wallabies and plenty of bird life around.

Staff was very welcoming and a great fish and chip (and pizza) shop just walking distance.

The beaches are just wonderful and accept your dogs.
Great spot. Clean and tidy. Excellent amenities. Right at the beach. Nice quiet area. Great service
Such a spacious park, close to the beach. Amazing service. They let us wait out the day before we catched the boat. Definitely will be back.
Excellent services, great spot.  Highly recommend

Fantastic spot, people in the area are so nice and polite.
My daughter loved the beach and the shared walkway which she could ride her bike along, just a really great spot

Absolutely amazing park right on the water, we had the pleasure of dealing with Drew (the caretaker) and his wife and they are amazing people who go above and beyond for their guests. The grounds itself and amenities were immaculate. Great rates and a park for the kids to play in. Would definitely recommend staying here 🙂
Perfect four night stay at this amazing place.  Highly recommend.

We have had the nicest stay at this gorgeous park! Staff are awesome! The park is so well maintained and the amenities are always clean. The sites are well set out and are a good size. The location is spot on!

Great location right on the beach which is dog friendly. Sealed bike track right out front. Great communication prior to check in and friendly host upon arrival. Clean and modern facilities and well kept park for our stay. Will be definetly back for a longer stay!
I have stayed several times now and is a firm favourite. The park is well run, amenities are very clean and the managers are very friendly and polite.
Very modern, neat and tidy, and the staff were very friendly couldn’t fault the cabin.

Excellent amenities - HIGHLY recommend paying for an ensuite powered site. Worth every penny! And the team at Reception were so lovely and helpful.

Great location too - just a few minutes from Port Arthur Historic Site.

Fabulous, we booked hut 3,  peninsula cabin, woke to great view.
Good heating and central location for attractions.
Watch out for wallabies

I rang up and booked in and they knew I'd be checking in late as I did the ghost tour they also knew I'm a woman on my own in a van and need to use the amenities and put me in a spot 50 metres away from it when others were much closer..was alot to pay for a unpowered site for the night.. only the one amenities block in middle closer to powered sites.
We stayed in a stargazing tent and loved it!! Plenty of space for all of us. The kids love their own mini tent while parents enjoy their outdoor bathtub!! Will stay longer next time 😍

Finally a caravan/camp ground with all the mod-cons (including a wood fired pizza oven) that still feels like you're camping and not staying in a resort. Great location, great facilities and friendly, helpful staff. A gem of a place, we'll be back next time.

We stayed on a powered site over Easter weekend.  This park is amazing, huge camp sites with own fire pit. Spotless amenities, huge camp kitchen and lounge room with huge open fire. Very friendly and helpful staff. The management put on fantastic Easter activities for the kids and a barbie on Sunday morning for everyone. Thank you and we will recommend your park.
Great place.

5 minutes from Port Arthur historic site.
Great facilities and good sized lots.
Nice walk down to Stewart's Bay Beach

This place is awesome! Loved staying in the stargazing safari tent. Very comfy beds, good heating and cooling available, just lovely. There is so much wildlife around, it was a treat watching the little wallabies and colourful birds from our deck. You couldn't ask for a better location, right on gorgeous Stewart's Bay and a 5 minute drive from everything. I hope I can come back one day.
Try to avoid this place. The service is terrible. Even Discriminatory. You guys know what I am talking about. Much better places in this beautiful area.
The service can't get any worse. Try to avoid this place when visiting Port Arthur area. Absolutely terrible experience.
Nice place and so close to Port Arthur heritage site.

Great caravan park with large drive through sites, set in bushland and excellent  facilities .  Short walk to a beautiful beach and short drive to Port Arthur.  Great place to base yourself to explore the Tasman Peninsula
Great location, clean ammenities and manager Michelle was extremely friendly and helpful.
Nice facilities, nice spot.  Very private.
A magnificent park in the best location. The most beautiful walk from here past Stewart’s Bay to Port Arthur jetty is just over 2kms and saves having to go thru the queues at front entrance.
Nice clean place.

We love visiting Port Arthur NRMA. The cabins and safari tents have everything we need for a weekend getaway. The location is perfect a stone throw away from Stewart’s bay and hikes on your doorstep. Recommend for families and couples.

We booked a self-contained cabin for 3 days. There was everything we needed to self cater. Cabin was compact & clean . There was a walking track to the beautiful Stewart Bay beach. Very close to the Port Arthur historic site. Staff were helpful & friendly
Great little spot close by to some nice beaches and of course the historic site. Lots of little wallabies around and they offer food so you can feed them!!
Stayed in a glamping cabin. Such a great experience.  Very clean and lovely service. Lots of birds and wildlife.
Has an extensive kitchen with pots, pans, fridge and great bathroom in solid brick buildings in centre of camp. Short walk from to beautiful beach- Stewards Bay Beach.
Another bush setting with room to move.
Life changing

A 30 min walk around the bays to the Port Arthur Historic site. Reception has a selection of groceries and frozen meals. The WiFi provided is patchy and slow (probably as a result of many users). The camp kitchen is excellent and there are multiple BBQs for resident use.

Great campsite. Clean and well looked after amenities. Would return.

We did request a site close to the amenities block and with drive in/ out access (which they have plenty of) as we were doing the Port Arthur night tour and we didn’t want to wake other campers up with our loud beeping when we reversed. Neither request was met during our stay. We were down the back of the park. Furtherest from the amenities block.

We recently had a week camping at Port Arthur Holiday Park, which we do annually. The management and staff this year were fantastic, the cleanliness of all facilities was above and beyond, and compliment the amazing location perfectly
Booked the wrong date they were packed out managed to fit me in. Facilities were nice and it was a rather large site.
Fantastic stay. Best shower I’ve ever had in a caravan park & 5 min walk to beautiful Stewart Bay beach

We stayed two nights in a Safari Stargazing Tent. This is proper glamping, a really nicely laid out space and the shower was incredible - the best we had the whole trip! Would have liked a few more cooking utensils and a bigger pan - cooking for any more than 2 would have been a challenge. Beds were comfy and the heater worked very well; the place was lovely and cosy after a day walking.
Great living spaces, great public area and great beach with a white Sunday view. Overall really great place.

What a beautiful caravan park !
I love the layout of all the different sites and it's natural beauty and great accessibility to the beach Amenities are spotless and modern and Dean the park Manager is great to deal with and gave us lots of information about the park and surrounding areas .  Thanks for a wonderful weekend and we will be back soon
Always a fantastic stay at Port Sorell Caravan Park, right on the beach, very clean amenities. Caretakers are very organised, great communication, very helpful. Pets allowed
Couldn't fault the park or the service
Great place to stay. Great friendly welcome
Very clean amenities
Great location and very clean amenities
Great park, fantastic caretakers !!
Great caravan park. Plenty of space for caravans and good area for kids to play. Great toilet and shower facilities. Very clean.
Flat site, close to amenities. Clean and very welcoming

This campground was a great place to stay. New amenities, big grassy sites so close to the water. The owners were so helpful and gave us a late check out as we had to catch the night sail on Spirit. Highly recommend.
Great park, friendly host, will stay there again.

We were in a caravan and not room, found the whole experience wonderful. Must note my partner did find the toilet shower amenities to be a little dated, no where to put cloths down in the shower room, no external seat to get undressed and redressed, although clean.
Nice, clean facilities. The site was spacious and flat. Office staff were friendly, welcoming and thorough.

Top location by the beach and to visit surrounding areas. Very good amenities and kept nice and clean plus the management allowed us to stay later on site so we could head straight to the Spirit of Tasmania.
Great park with New amenities friendly caretaker

Beautiful location, roomy powered site, great estuary beach access, clean user friendly facilities. Caravan park manager made checkin and out a breeze and stayed in contact via text message and responded promptly to questions. Highly recommend staying here!
Always a great caravan park.
Yes grate stay

The staff and managers are absolutely amazing and the scenery is beautiful. The park is very fun but could use some better equipment. Overall love everything about the campsite and love the staff a lot!

Thank you for a lovely stay!
Stayed for 2 nights, and didn't have anything to complain about.
We stayed in a Waterview Cabin, it was fantastic 👏

We had a nice time, it was quiet comfortable the garden cabin and park it’s self nice. Disappointed with washing machines not working, cost of wood expensive for open fires, wish there was accessible shops as reception only sells little essentials.

We booked 7 nights and extended to 10 on a powered caravan site which is very well maintained large level with own fire pit, by far the best Holiday Park we stayed in to date. Managers and maintenance staff are very friendly courteous and always there to assist if need be. Amenities are excellent modern and clean along with camp kitchen BBQ facilities games rooms and a comprehensive children's playground. Only a few minutes walk along the beach to Port Arthur Historic Settlement and a short drive to Port Arthur township which has a general store with good takeaway and limited but good staples on shelves. Fuel pumps both Diesel and unleaded in front.

Big non-powered sites. Well spaced from neighbours. Big kitchen, multiple outdoor bbqs under cover with their own sitting area, bmx ramps, bike repair station, playground, walking distance to Port Arthur historic site via a trail, playground and you can hear the waves at night. Michelle and Dan keep campers updated through text of changes. They have a kids activity at least once a week, and I just overheard Dan saying he gets up every morning at 3 am to check the bathrooms. Cute poteroos around.
Very clean rooms
One of the nicest cabins I have ever stayed in. Fully detached, spotlessly clean, well equiped and maintained in a beautiful bushy setting.

Huge park with a beautiful walk to the historical sites (although I only walked 5 minutes up to a white sand beach). Our cabin was very spacious, updated, distant from nearby cabins, and frequented by wallabies and rosellas.

We have been wanting to go to Port Arthur and Tasman National Park for a long time. We staked out airbnb, local hotels and others, but found NRMA Port Arthur Holiday Park had the best affordable accommodation there. When we arrived, we had a powered tent site which was a good size. We were a a couple of minutes walk to the bathrooms and kitchen. The amenities were amazing! Their shared kitchen has everything you’ll need. They even have a pizza oven, which you can request to use.
We had family that got the bunkhouses and they were great. They housed 9 people and had a fridge for personal use.
The staff were amazing. They were even willing to help in sending our stuff back that we accidentally left. They were always courteous and considerate.
We absolutely loved our time at the Holiday Park. Being just minutes from Port Arthur and not far from some amazing other sites, makes this place a must!

Big, spacious park. Had the powered site for two nights. Place is good, functional. Plenty of clean toilets and bathrooms.
Laundry room is abit disappointing as out of 3 washing machines, only one is working. But all dryers are functional.
Clean & spacious bbq area. There’s a dining room nearby.
Wonderful children playground and a skate park for the kids too.

We stayed at the garden cabin and first time staying in holiday park. It had everything we needed and shower pressure (plus great hot water) were perfect after long days of hike! The only down thing was there was only 1 washer available out of 3 so had to wait for long. Otherwise I give 2 thumbs up and would def come back!
Great get away
The managers are very friendly and helpful. Pademelons roam freely around the park and the proximity to all the Tasman Peninsula tourist attractions makes this accommodation a great choice!

We had an unpowered site which was good, the bathrooms where good, however the showers where very small.

Good kitchen and laundry facilities.
מוזר....ביקשו מאיתנו לסדר את המצעים למיטה הנוספת

Best park we have stayed in while travelling around Tassie.  Would come back here again and stay longer rather than just overnight.  Apparently you can go along a walking track to Port Arthur, takes 50 minutes.
We had a cottage that had wheelchair access throughout.
All round hoghly recoomend thos park to everyone. Wonderful staff, facilities and grounds! Only a couple minutes drive from Port Arthur historic site.
Stayed two nights in the dorm whilst visiting area. Very affordable met lots of nice people and staff. Lots of parking and nice surroundings.
Cook have had some sort of cooktop in the room very quiet with nature right at you're door
They ripped me off by padding EFTPoS transactions over the counter today. Beware.
Absolutely loved it here! Great big sites in a natural setting. Wildlife everywhere and an interesting park just to wander around in. Great camp kitchen and lounge. Could have stayed for a week.
Really good
Well located and reasonably priced
Full facility and fire pit noise is no problems as family at all time. Feel the forest with friendly birds and animals

Was a pretty good campsite, great location, nice and quiet when we were there. Fire pits were included , and it was a lovely night stay. Cool to see all the potaroos pademellons and possums about too. 1 draw was the 5 mins shower limit, but it is what it is
Best holiday park we have visited. We had the waterview cabin and it was so relaxing. Not cramped in like a lot of other parks. Heaps of wildlife, very quiet. Highly recommended!
We had the powered safari style accommodation. Fantastic. The outdoor bath was such a novelty and added to our experience.

We stayed at a stargazing tent as a family of 4, and we are so glad that we did. It was lovely, well equipped, and clean. Note that there is no "stove or oven" in the tent, but there is a bbq on the tents balcony.
My favourite part of the stay was feeding the parrot's that desend from everywhere!

We stayed with a motorhome the first day arriving in Tasmania, we loved it so much we spent another two nights at the end of our trip as a last hurrah. The place is beautiful with pademelons appearing late in the afternoon and very early morning. You will get a close experience with the local currawongs and cheeky rosellas.

The staff were amazing, on our return we were going to arrive after hours, they were very forgiving about it and allowed us to collect our access to the park in the after hours drop off. Absolutely awesome service from them.
Loved it. Will stay again.
Fantastic Stargazing Tent. Beautiful inside and views were spectacular

Stayed for 3 nights here in a fantastic setting. Park management and staff were good to deal with.
Unfortunately our room need some work. Cupboard doors falling off our bathroom door was difficult to use the shower rose and towel rail were loose.
After approaching staff we were informed that new management had just taken over and were working through the issues.
I wish them luck with their new endeavours and hope they can make improvements to the park.
We only stayed a night in our motor home. But wow what an amazing place , so clean and the service is fantastic,  we will definitely be back for a longer stay , thank you 😊

The caravan park is very well kept and staff are very friendly and helpful. Unfortunately we had two nights with hardly any sleep because of inconsiderate people that don’t know when to stop playing music and being loud till 2:30 in the morning. We decided to leave early and the owner gave us two nights credit. We will definitely return and hopefully have better neighbours

Beautiful location and park is very well kept.
We had a spacious unpowered site that was the perfect size for our large tent and gazebo.
I highly recommend you stay for longer than 2 nights to enjoy everything that is on offer in the local area!
Great park, very family friendly and pets welcome. Had great site and Amenities clean and spacious. Reception very friendly and helpful. Will be back soon.

Absolutely fantastic caravan park, the location is great.
Very friendly at the check in.
Great caravan park, good location. Very organised and well maintained.
Large sites and great atmosphere and amenities
